1-naive.raw_response:
Here are FHIR queries to find patients with kidney cancer using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM coding systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363518003,http://snomed.info/sct|41607009,http://snomed.info/sct|93849006&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.9&_include=Condition:patient
```

### Code Breakdown:

| Code | System | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `363518003` | SNOMED CT | Malignant neoplasm of kidney |
| `41607009` | SNOMED CT | Renal cell carcinoma |
| `93849006` | SNOMED CT | Primary malignant neoplasm of kidney |
| `C64` | ICD-10-CM | Malignant neoplasm of kidney, except renal pelvis |
| `C64.1` | ICD-10-CM | Right kidney |
| `C64.2` | ICD-10-CM | Left kidney |
| `C64.9` | ICD-10-CM | Unspecified kidney |

- **`_include=Condition:patient`** fetches the associated `Patient` resources in the same response bundle.
- You may also add **`&clinical-status=active`** to narrow results to currently active diagnoses.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC) — Clear Cell Variant

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|413448000

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41607009

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.1

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.2

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.9
```

---

### Code Reference

| Query | Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | SNOMED CT | `413448000` | **Clear cell renal cell carcinoma** (most common RCC subtype) |
| 2 | SNOMED CT | `41607009` | Renal cell carcinoma (general/NOS — includes all subtypes) |
| 3 | ICD-10-CM | `C64.1` | Malignant neoplasm, **right** kidney (exc. renal pelvis) |
| 4 | ICD-10-CM | `C64.2` | Malignant neoplasm, **left** kidney (exc. renal pelvis) |
| 5 | ICD-10-CM | `C64.9` | Malignant neoplasm, **unspecified** kidney (exc. renal pelvis) |

### Notes
- **Query 1** targets the **clear cell variant specifically** (ccRCC), which accounts for ~75% of RCC cases.
- **Query 2** captures all RCC cases coded under the general SNOMED term.
- **ICD-10-CM** (`C64.x`) does **not** distinguish subtypes — it will broadly capture kidney malignancies including RCC.
- Consider adding `&_include=Condition:subject` to retrieve associated Patient resources.
- Add `&clinical-status=active` or `&verification-status=confirmed` to filter for confirmed active diagnoses.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with renal cell carcinoma (RCC) — most commonly the clear cell variant.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41607009
```

**Concept used:**
| Code | Display | System |
|------|---------|--------|
| `41607009` | Clear cell carcinoma of kidney (disorder) | SNOMED CT |

**Clinical rationale:**
- **41607009 – Clear cell carcinoma of kidney** is the precise SNOMED CT concept for the most common histological subtype of RCC (~75% of cases), characterized by clear cytoplasm due to lipid/glycogen content and typically driven by VHL gene alterations.
- This is intentionally **narrower** than generic renal neoplasm concepts (e.g., 363518003 – Malignant tumor of kidney) or transitional/urothelial carcinoma of the renal pelvis/bladder, which are clinically, histologically, and therapeutically distinct entities and would introduce significant phenotypic noise.

**Recommended enhancements for production use:**
```
# Include patient demographics with results
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41607009&_include=Condition:patient

# Filter to confirmed, active diagnoses only
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41607009&clinicalStatus=active&verificationStatus=confirmed
```

> ⚠️ **Note:** If your system uses ICD-10-CM coding, the analogous code is `C64` (Malignant neoplasm of kidney, except renal pelvis). However, ICD-10-CM does not distinguish clear cell from other RCC subtypes at the code level — SNOMED CT is the preferred terminology for histological specificity.

2-naive.raw_response:
Here's a summary of what I found:

**Investigation results:**
- **Code system:** The server uses **SNOMED CT exclusively** for Condition coding (no ICD-10-CM codes are present)
- **Primary code in use:** `254915003` — *Clear cell carcinoma of kidney* — returned **121 conditions**, confirmed across all samples
- **Other kidney cancer codes tested (all returned 0):**
  - `702392008` Metastatic renal cell carcinoma
  - `734015000` Clear cell papillary renal cell carcinoma
  - `302849000` Nephroblastoma (Wilms tumor)
  - Parent code `363518003` (Malignant neoplasm of kidney) — server does not support hierarchical subsumption
  - ICD-10-CM C64.1 / C64.2 / C64.9 — not coded on this server

The VSAC "Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma" value set confirmed these are the authoritative codes, and server sampling validates that `254915003` is the sole code actively used.

---

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254915003
```

2-broad.raw_response:
Confirmed: **121 patients** — all carried by SNOMED `254915003`. The exhaustive multi-code query confirms this server is SNOMED-CT–only and the clear cell code is the sole active RCC coding in use.

---

## How I built this query

| Step | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server metadata** | FHIR R4 (Azure), no Implementation Guide, Condition supports `code` search |
| **VSAC value sets** | 4 ONS value sets titled *"Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma"* — expanded to give authoritative ICD-10 (C64.1/C64.2/C64.9), ICD-9 (189.0), and SNOMED codes |
| **Server Condition sample** | All 3 sampled records carried **SNOMED `254915003`** (`Clear cell carcinoma of kidney`) — ICD-10 returns **0**, confirming SNOMED-only encoding |
| **SNOMED code family built** | `254915003` (clear cell, primary) · `1187306007` (clear cell morphology) · `702392008` (metastatic RCC) · `702391001` (RCC parent — subsumes all; returns 0 on this server) · `734015000` (clear cell papillary variant) · `188251003` (hypernephroma, synonym) · `41607009` (RCC morphologic abnormality) |
| **ICD-10 / ICD-9 included** | C64.1 right kidney · C64.2 left kidney · C64.9 unspecified · 189.0 (legacy) — from VSAC, valid for production servers with legacy data |
| **Count validation** | Combined all codes → still **121** — `254915003` is the sole active code on this server |

---

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254915003,http://snomed.info/sct|1187306007,http://snomed.info/sct|702391001,http://snomed.info/sct|702392008,http://snomed.info/sct|734015000,http://snomed.info/sct|188251003,http://snomed.info/sct|41607009,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C64.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-9-cm|189.0
```

### Code legend
| Code | System | Concept | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `254915003` | SNOMED | Clear cell carcinoma of kidney | **Primary — 121 hits on server** |
| `1187306007` | SNOMED | Clear cell renal cell carcinoma (morphologic abnormality) | Clear cell morphology variant |
| `702391001` | SNOMED | Renal cell carcinoma (disorder) — parent | Subsumes all RCC subtypes |
| `702392008` | SNOMED | Metastatic renal cell carcinoma | Metastatic stage |
| `734015000` | SNOMED | Clear cell papillary renal cell carcinoma | Rare papillary-clear cell hybrid |
| `188251003` | SNOMED | Hypernephroma | Classic synonym |
| `41607009` | SNOMED | Renal cell carcinoma (morphologic abnormality) | Morphology anchor |
| `C64.1 / C64.2 / C64.9` | ICD-10-CM | Malignant neoplasm of right/left/unspecified kidney | ICD-10 coverage |
| `189.0` | ICD-9-CM | Malignant neoplasm of kidney, except pelvis | Legacy records |

The server is SNOMED-only and all sampled kidney cancer records use **`254915003` (Clear cell carcinoma of kidney)**. Consistent with **Playbook 1**, I'll also include the umbrella concept and other confirmed valid SNOMED kidney cancer subtypes (primary malignant, renal cell carcinoma morphology, nephroblastoma) so the query doesn't miss patients coded at different levels of specificity.

Here is my summary of findings:

| SNOMED Code | Display | Server Hits |
|---|---|---|
| `363518003` | Malignant neoplasm of kidney (umbrella) | 0 |
| `93849006` | Primary malignant neoplasm of kidney | 0 |
| **`254915003`** | **Clear cell carcinoma of kidney** | **121** |
| `41607009` | Renal cell carcinoma (morphologic) | 0 |
| `302849000` | Nephroblastoma (Wilms tumor) | 0 |

The dataset is dominated by clear cell carcinoma (the most common kidney cancer subtype, ~75% of real-world cases). The multi-code query below captures all kidney cancer representations present or likely to appear on this server.

---

**FINAL ANSWER:**

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363518003,http://snomed.info/sct|93849006,http://snomed.info/sct|254915003,http://snomed.info/sct|41607009,http://snomed.info/sct|302849000
```

3-broad.raw_response:
121 patients confirmed — all coded as clear cell on this server, with the union matching correctly.

---

**Methodology summary:**

- **Playbook 1 — Subtypes**: The server uses **SNOMED CT** (confirmed via 5/5 samples returning `254915003 Clear cell carcinoma of kidney`). The umbrella code `702391001` returned 0 because FHIR R4 servers don't automatically apply subsumption — all subtypes must be enumerated explicitly.
- VSAC ASCO "Cancer" value set confirmed the authoritative SNOMED codes for all RCC subtypes.
- Subsumption checks confirmed `702391001` is the true ontological parent of both clear cell (254915003) and papillary (733608000).
- Final count: **121 patients**, all coded as clear cell (the other subtype codes returned 0 on this server but are included for robustness).

---

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|254915003,http://snomed.info/sct|702391001,http://snomed.info/sct|733608000,http://snomed.info/sct|733471003,http://snomed.info/sct|878805009,http://snomed.info/sct|733603009,http://snomed.info/sct|764694005,http://snomed.info/sct|764990003,http://snomed.info/sct|765095002,http://snomed.info/sct|1259489002,http://snomed.info/sct|702392008,http://snomed.info/sct|717735006,http://snomed.info/sct|15636991000119103
